steps.title: Identify what the blank must do
steps.content: Sentence 1 sets up a world where companies own and sell breathable air. Sentence 2 states that people must purchase it. The blank must connect a cause (companies selling air) to its direct consequence (people buying it).

steps.title: Eliminate each choice and pick the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'As a result,': Signals that sentence 2 is the logical consequence of sentence 1 — exactly the cause-and-effect link needed. ✓
  • Choice B — 'In any case,': Means “no matter what the situation is,” implying multiple scenarios exist; only one scenario is described here. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Nevertheless,': Signals contradiction or concession — the second sentence does not contradict the first. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Earlier,': Signals a shift back in time; both sentences describe the same fictional present. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
